title: Nursery Rhyme. CLXI. Songs.
content:
Wooley Foster has gone to sea,
        With silver buckles at his knee,
        When he comes back he'll marry me, - 
                Bonny Wooley Foster!

        Wooley Foster has a cow,
        Black and white about the mow,
        Open the gates and let her through,
                Wooley Foster's ain cow!

        Wooley Foster has a hen,
        Cockle button, cockle ben,
        She lay eggs for gentlemen,
                But none for Wooley Foster!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CLXI. Songs.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
